title: Today's AI Brief: Gemini 3.5 and AI in Admin
summary: Google's new Gemini 3.5 model and AI in admin departments
imageAlt: Image from source article for Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action

body.text: I'm catching up on some fascinating AI developments. Google just dropped Gemini 3.5, their latest model that's all about executing complex workflows. This is part of a bigger trend of AI models leveling up and becoming more autonomous. It's exciting to see how these advancements will play out in real-world scenarios.

Gemini 3.5 is built to handle agentic workflows, which means it can tackle tasks that typically require human-like intelligence and action. According to Google DeepMind's CTO Koray Kavukcuoglu, this model is a significant step forward in AI capabilities. I'm curious to see how this will be applied in everyday situations.

In other news, AI is making its way into small business admin departments. An article from MIT Technology Review highlights how AI can take over tasks such as accounting, design, and market research. For small businesses, this could be a total game-changer. No more tedious admin work, and more time for the fun stuff.

Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

The Download: AI can run your admin department now is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: This is today's edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what's going on in the world of technology. How small businesses can leverage AI From accounting to design to market research and product development, there’s a staggering breadth of skills needed to run a business. Large companies can hire experts to handle these... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
